text.title: Definition Of Ready Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for definition-of-ready-checklist. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: A story is READY only when all 8 items pass: clear outcome, named acceptance criteria, design or wireframe linked, dependencies resolved, env access granted, test data identified, security/legal sign-off (if applicable), client PM accountable; less than or equal to 7/8 equals NOT READY and routes back to refinement.

rationale.source: PMBOK 7 + IIBA BABOK v3 readiness criteria

rule.rationale: PMBOK 7 + IIBA BABOK v3 readiness criteria — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.

rule.statement: Client-side dependency check (5 items) is mandatory and runs BEFORE engineering check: (a) client PM confirmed in writing, (b) env credentials or VPN tested by a team member, (c) realistic test dataset accessible, (d) regulator/legal/security ticket exists or is N/A in writing, (e) any required client-side IT change is ticketed with a stated SLA.
rule.rationale: Outsource delivery anti-patterns (BABOK perspective) — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.

rule.statement: Stories carrying compliance impact (HIPAA, GDPR, SOC2, PCI) require a named compliance contact and a written N/A or sign-off attached; without it, ready=false regardless of other items.
rule.rationale: HIPAA / GDPR practitioner guidance — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.

rule.statement: DoR check is timeboxed to 5 minutes per story in grooming; if the story cannot be evaluated in 5 minutes, mark NOT READY for 'insufficient definition' and route to a dedicated discovery session.
rule.rationale: Workshop facilitation research — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.

rule.statement: Every DoR failure is logged with the failing item id; the top failing item per client is the discovery focus for the next iteration.
rule.rationale: Continuous improvement (Lean) — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.

rule.statement: Re-estimation of the same story is forbidden until the prior DoR failure has been cleared in writing; this enforces upstream work and removes the false comfort of re-estimating a blocked story.
rule.rationale: Agile estimation discipline — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.

rule.statement: The checklist is co-owned by BA + PM and reviewed quarterly with the client; clients who consistently produce DoR failures get a written escalation in the QBR — DoR is the client-side accountability artefact.
rule.rationale: Client governance / QBR practice — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.

rule.statement: If the team has no client-side dependencies at all or operates entirely in-house, return `skipped` — methodology overhead does not pay back.
rule.rationale: Methodology applicability — applies to Definition Of Ready Checklist.
